# Phase 0 baseline — forensic record
#
# Closure-plan G.1 deliverable. This file is the canonical pre-summit
# reference for Metric 2's per-backend "no regression vs Phase 0" gate.
# It is committed (gitignore relaxed) so the audit trail survives
# rebases. The companion live-measurement file is metric2.txt; this
# phase0.txt is read by humans and by the per-record `phase0=` field
# in metric2.txt — not parsed by any test.
#
# ============================================================================
# Pre-summit SHA
# ============================================================================
#
#   canonical: 11b8a53  bus32: pin high-backing contract for IE32/x86/bare-M68K
#   selection: first parent of c6f324c (the JIT-summit consolidation
#              commit), per the closure-plan G.1 procedure step 1.
#   verified: `git log --oneline --first-parent c6f324c~1` resolves to
#             11b8a53 on the JITparity branch as of 2026-05-01.
#
# ============================================================================
# Instrumentation audit at pre-summit
# ============================================================================
#
# Plan G.1 step 4 required verifying whether pre-summit jit_*_exec.go
# carry the JIT-bound timing hooks (perfAcct.AddJit / AddInterp) used
# by Metric 2's condition 3 ("≥10% improvement on JIT-bound time vs
# Phase 0"). Audit on 2026-05-01:
#
#   pre-summit (11b8a53):
#     git show 11b8a53:cpu_x86.go    | grep perfAcct  →  0 matches
#     git show 11b8a53:jit_*_exec.go | grep perfAcct  →  0 matches
#     Only perf-reporting MIPS print loop present (time.Now usage is
#     for the once-per-second instructions/sec print, not JIT-bound
#     accounting).
#
#   post-summit (HEAD):
#     perfAcct.AddJit / AddInterp / AddInstrs wired across all five
#     CPU exec loops; condition-3 measurable.
#
# Plan-documented consequence: every Metric 2 record waives condition
# 3 with reason `pre-summit-no-instrumentation` (closure-plan G.1
# step 4 fallback, "Backporting hooks into pre-summit is explicitly
# out of scope"). Conditions 1 (no regression vs Phase 0 wall-clock)
# and 2 (reaches real-time) remain measurable and binding.
#
# ============================================================================
# Per-workload Phase-0 availability registry
# ============================================================================
#
# Plan G.1 step 3 required that each Metric 2 workload run from a
# binary present at the pre-summit SHA, else it gates under a per-
# backend waiver ("where a backend has no canonical real workload
# in-tree, Phase 1 selects one").
#
#   x86       : rotozoomer_gem.prg PRESENT at 11b8a53
#               (sdk/examples/prebuilt/rotozoomer_gem.prg). Note: the
#               pre-summit binary executes on the M68K profile under
#               EmuTOS; the post-summit x86-native rotozoomer JIT
#               path landed in c6f324c. Cross-profile comparison is
#               apples-to-oranges — the x86 record below waives
#               Phase-0 wall-clock with reason
#               `no-pre-summit-x86-native-workload`.
#
#   m68k      : aros-ie.rom + rotozoomer_gem.prg PRESENT at 11b8a53.
#               Canonical workload candidate: AROS boot to first idle
#               frame (per plan §1). Real wall-clock measurable on
#               both SHAs.
#
#   z80       : NO canonical Spectrum demo binary at 11b8a53. The
#               sdk/ tree at pre-summit has zero .tap / .sna / Z80
#               native binaries. Z80 record waives condition 1 with
#               reason `no-pre-summit-z80-workload-binary`.
#
#   6502      : `build_6502_benchmarks.sh` + `6502_bench.test` PRESENT
#               at 11b8a53 (verified via
#               `git ls-tree -r --name-only 11b8a53 | grep 6502`).
#               Those are the JIT microbenchmark harness (per-opcode
#               throughput sweep), NOT a Phase-1 "representative
#               C64/NES diag program" workload — plan §1 explicitly
#               distinguishes "real workload" from microbench. The
#               6502 record waives condition 1 with reason
#               `no-pre-summit-6502-real-workload` (microbench
#               present, but no canonical user-facing demo binary
#               that exercises the full bus + chip wiring path
#               Metric 2 measures).
#
#   ie64      : ie_product_demo.ies PRESENT at 11b8a53. Real wall-clock
#               measurable on both SHAs but only via the IEScript
#               driver — no ie64-native JIT path at pre-summit (IE64
#               JIT widening landed mid-summit per the cb5a16a series).
#               IE64 record measures wall-clock end-to-end across the
#               script driver, which captures the user-visible cost
#               and is gate-meaningful even without per-backend native
#               JIT bound timing.
#
# ============================================================================
# Reconstruction status (2026-05-01)
# ============================================================================
#
# The Phase-0 reconstruction worktree (`git worktree add /tmp/ie-phase0
# 11b8a53`) and the 3×30s wall-clock capture per workload remain user-
# driven. The closure-plan addendum records this as the documented
# manual step — the bench harness cannot run autonomously inside a
# write-bound CI agent without a clean mains-power machine and a
# headless Vulkan/Ebiten stack. Live measurements drop into
# metric2.txt (gitignored relax: now committed) under the per-record
# `phase0=` field, with `io_bound_waiver=true` +
# `waiver_reason=pre-summit-no-instrumentation` on every record per
# the audit above.
#
# Until those measurements land, TestBenchUniformity_Metric2 skips
# (gate test contract: `metric2.txt absent → skip`). On a clean clone
# the gate cannot perf-fail, which matches the reviewer-proof contract
# documented in README.md.
